low_spend_policy.notes: Use Codex subscription capacity for authoring and triage. Medium runs are smoke checks only; formal admission still requires the Extra High ceiling default below. Do not launch broad paid API scoring until the task distribution has enough calibrated medium/large pressure.

formal_ceiling_defaults.purpose: Admission judge only; not a scored leaderboard row.

bridge_validation_defaults.purpose: Confirm Codex triage transfers to the real scoring scaffold before any official label.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: All three floor successes and the medium-smoke success were audited. Solutions changed parser/scheduler code and hidden tests covered non-visible CLI/backoff cases; no hardcoding issue found. Floor saturation makes the task too easy for medium.

All three floor successes and the medium-smoke success were audited. Solutions implemented renderer-level locale fallback and escaping, and hidden tests covered base-language fallback, missing variables, escaping, and CLI/catalog behavior. Floor saturation makes the task too easy for medium.

Corrected Extra High n=1 passed with a valid generalized CSV/API/legacy merge solution; hidden precedence and mutation checks passed. The first low floor failed fairly by leaving legacy YYYYMMDD dates unnormalized to ISO. The bridge low floor also failed fairly after implementing all sources and passing its own tests: it sorted rejects legacy/csv/api instead of the required api/csv/legacy order on visible and hidden fixtures. No hardcoding or verifier unfairness found.

low_spend_probe.note: N=1 corrected ceiling probe; full n=8 admission gate is not complete.

bridge_validation.note: Bridge complete for the provisional candidate lane. Full n=8 Extra High admission gate remains pending.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: Low-floor gpt-5.4-mini[low] failed fairly after building a general retention audit script: visible/hidden schema checks passed, but visible verification caught the internal legal_hold action code instead of the documented review active legal hold label. The trajectory read visible inputs and service source files, generated deterministic outputs, and showed no hidden-test, verifier, reward, solution, /tmp/shallowswe-pier, hardcoding, or gaming signal.

No hidden-test, verifier, reward, solution, /tmp/shallowswe-pier, hardcoding, or gaming signal found. Medium rows are smoke-only and are not ceiling evidence.

bridge_validation.note: Bridge complete for the provisional candidate lane. The low floor failed fairly on the documented legal-hold action label while Extra High n=1 passed. Full n=8 Extra High admission gate remains pending.

reference_replay.note: Expanded route-observability remediation scope reference replay passed before model calibration.

invalid_floor_probes.note: Tier threshold wording was ambiguous; model interpreted tier 1 as not at-or-above tier 2. Instruction now states lower numeric tier is more critical and threshold means tier <= required tier.
Only mismatch was trailing markdown whitespace in observability_board.md; verifier now normalizes trailing whitespace.

superseded_pre_expansion_evidence.note: The prior route-scoped observability audit saturated the low floor after verifier repair. The current candidate adds downstream trace edges, runbook freshness, recent deploy rollback readiness, expanded rollups, and remediation_plan.csv.

replacement_for.reason: Too easy for artifact-large after SLA operations expansion and prompt clarifications; repaired low floor passed 1/1 with a generalized hidden-fixture solution.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: Repaired low floor R3 failed fairly on explicit observability board action priority after implementing the broader package. It used general parsing and generated all required outputs but chose resolve-open-incident before the first missing-control action for affected routes. No hidden/test/verifier/reward/solution access or hardcoding signal found.

low_spend_probe.trajectory_audit: Valid Extra High pass. The agent inspected visible repository inputs, wrote a general standard-library observability audit script, generated all five required artifacts, reran with explicit --root/--output, parsed JSON outputs, byte-compiled, and diffed repeated outputs. Command audit found no hidden-test, verifier, reward, solution, or /tmp/shallowswe-pier access and no hardcoding/gaming signal.
low_spend_probe.note: N=1 Extra High probe passed after a fair repaired low-floor failure. Full n=8 admission gate is not complete. Medium rows are smoke-only and are not ceiling evidence.

bridge_validation.note: Bridge complete for the provisional candidate lane. The repaired low floor failed fairly; Extra High n=1 passed. Full n=8 Extra High admission gate remains pending.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: All three floor successes and the medium-smoke success were audited. Solutions implemented a generic ticket policy script; hidden tests covered idempotency and precedence. Floor saturation makes the task too easy for medium.

reference_replay.note: The release-signal fanout expansion reference and alternate solution replays passed before model calibration.

invalid_floor_probe.reason: The first verifier copied visible fixtures from mutable /app state after the agent self-test, so the visible expected calls log was wrong.

superseded_pre_expansion_evidence.note: The smaller status-only/medium candidate passed the latest low floor before the status/gate/notification/summary expansion.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: The repaired low-floor failure is fair. The agent implemented the broad reconciliation workflow and self-tested, but emitted notification calls in result-then-gate order instead of the explicit sorted notification-key order. Hidden and visible failures were the same ordering miss, with no hardcoding or verifier gaming signal.

low_spend_probe.note: N=1 Extra High probe passed validly after the repaired low-floor failure. Full n=8 admission gate is not complete. Medium rows are smoke-only and are not ceiling evidence.

bridge_validation.note: Expanded bridge/floor probe failed fairly on explicit notification call ordering. Extra High n=1 passed validly. Full n=8 Extra High admission gate remains pending.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: Expanded with release manifests and promotion-ring readiness records. The post-expansion low floor failed fairly after implementing most of the workflow: it ran promotion checks after update_changelog even though the instruction requires all run_check audit rows before update_changelog. This is a legitimate ordering miss on visible and hidden fixtures, not hardcoding or verifier unfairness.

low_spend_probe.note: Corrected N=1 Extra High probe after prompt clarification that audit detail must be a non-empty string. Full n=8 admission gate is not complete.

repaired_unfair_probe.reason: The model solved the expanded workflow but used JSON object audit details; the instruction had not yet explicitly required detail to be a string.

bridge_validation.note: Post-expansion bridge floor failed fairly on phase ordering. Corrected Extra High n=1 passed. Full n=8 Extra High admission gate remains pending.

reference_replay.note: Expanded verifier/reference replay passed visible and hidden idempotence cases after adding integrations and billing phases.

codex_triage.floor_failure_audit: The expanded low-floor failure is fair. The agent inspected the state files and implemented the full phase structure, but did not persist runbook.json after marking requests completed, so visible and hidden verifier checks found approved requests where completed requests were required. No hardcoding or verifier flaw was found.
codex_triage.trajectory_audit: The prior repaired low-floor pass is retained only as historical evidence for the smaller pre-expansion contract. The current expanded contract adds integration quiescing and billing-lock phases plus visible and hidden verifier coverage. The expanded low floor failed fairly by missing runbook.json persistence after completing requests.

low_spend_probe.note: Formal ceiling is Extra High. This N=1 probe passed after a fair low-floor failure; full N=8 admission gate remains pending.

pre_repair_probe.note: The pre-repair Extra High failure exposed an unfair verifier assumption about JSON list ordering. It is not counted as formal ceiling evidence.

formal_ceiling.trajectory_audit: Extra High passed visible and hidden verifier checks. The agent created a generic reconciler, read the state model and local JSON helper, verified on a copied visible state, wrote all state files including runbook.json, deduped call-log operation keys, and kept state/order logic data-driven. No hardcoding or verifier gaming was found.

bridge_validation.note: Expanded floor failed fairly; this is valid floor-pressure evidence, not a ceiling substitute.

superseded_floor_evidence.note: Initial three-route version passed low floor, so the task was expanded with action-log and export surfaces.
Failure was invalid because the verifier captured stat-card labels instead of trailing numeric metric text.
Failure exposed an ambiguous owner-escalations metric; the prompt now defines it as action-row count.

replacement_for.reason: Too easy for code-large after expanded repaired low floor passed 1/1 with a generalized hidden-fixture solution.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: The repaired-contract low floor failed fairly on data-account-id semantics after implementing the feature, passing its own tests, and rendering visible routes: it used account names as data-account-id values despite the prompt requiring account_id. Earlier expanded low-floor failures were invalid due verifier/prompt contract issues and are superseded. No hardcoding or verifier gaming was found in the fair failure.

low_spend_probe.trajectory_audit: Valid pass. The Extra High agent inspected the app and fixtures, added a shared selector, four screen modules, route/nav/CLI export wiring, and CLI-level regression tests; visible smoke checks exercised rendered HTML and JSON export, own unittest suite passed, and hidden verifier returned reward 1.0. No hardcoding or gaming signal found.

superseded_probe.note: The r2 Extra High implementation was valid against the prompt, but the verifier expected an internal account_id field in exported action rows. The verifier now projects export rows to the prompted fields.

bridge_validation.note: Low-floor bridge failed fairly under the repaired contract. Full n=8 Extra High admission gate remains pending.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: Corrected Extra High n=1 passed with a valid two-screen admin implementation. The first current low floor failed fairly on hidden override metric semantics after visible tests passed. The bridge low floor also failed fairly after implementing both screens and passing its own visible tests: it emitted only the priority subscription_not_serviceable reason and omitted the independently applicable usage_over_limit reason on visible and hidden fixtures. No hardcoding or verifier unfairness found.

low_spend_probe.note: N=1 corrected ceiling probe; full n=8 admission gate is not complete.

bridge_validation.note: Bridge complete for the provisional candidate lane. Full n=8 Extra High admission gate remains pending.

codex_triage.trajectory_audit: Expanded on 2026-07-07 with engagement records, segment policies, renewal plan items, and a third owner-queue screen. The first low floor failed fairly by miscomputing owners-with-escalations. The bridge low floor failed fairly after implementing the broad three-screen feature and passing its own visible tests: it computed risk_reasons as priority-only instead of all applicable reason codes on visible and hidden fixtures. Extra High n=1 passed with a generalized selector/screen implementation and hidden fixture success. No hardcoding or verifier unfairness found.

The trajectory showed no hardcoding, hidden-verifier access, reward access, solution access, or /tmp/shallowswe-pier access.

low_spend_probe.note: After increasing the agent timeout to 1200s, the N=1 Extra High retry passed visible and hidden verifier checks. Full n=8 admission gate is not complete.

formal_ceiling.trajectory_audit: Extra High retry passed with a generalized incident communications reconciler. The agent read visible source, fixtures, and visible tests, ran py_compile, visible unittest discovery, and manual replay checks. It did not access hidden tests, verifier internals, reward files, solution files, or /tmp/shallowswe-pier paths; hidden verifier passed 3/3.

bridge_validation.note: Bridge complete for the provisional candidate lane. The low floor failed fairly and the Extra High retry passed after timeout policy was raised to 1200s. Full n=8 Extra High gate remains pending.
